lines.english: It is God's Nature to love His devotees.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿੰਦਕ ਮਾਰਿ ਚਰਨ ਤਲ ਦੀਨੇ ਅਪੁਨੋ ਜਸੁ ਵਰਤਾਇਓ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: nindak maar charan tal deene apuno jas varataaeo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He destroys the slanderers, crushing them beneath His Feet. His Glory is manifest everywhere. ||1||Pause||
Malaar, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈ ਜੈ ਕਾਰੁ ਕੀਨੋ ਸਭ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਦਇਆ ਜੀਅਨ ਮਹਿ ਪਾਇਓ ॥
lines.translit: jai jai kaar keeno sabh jag meh deaa jeean meh paaeo |
lines.english: His Victory is celebrated all throughout the world. He blesses all creatures with compassion.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਗੀਕਾਰੁ ਕੀਓ ਮੇਰੇ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਭ੍ਰਮੁ ਭਉ ਮੇਟਿ ਸੁਖਾਇਓ ॥
lines.translit: angeekaar keeo mere suaamee bhram bhau mett sukhaaeo |
lines.english: My Lord and Master has made me His Own; dispelling my doubts and fears, He has made me happy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਠਿ ਲਾਇ ਅਪੁਨੋ ਦਾਸੁ ਰਾਖਿਓ ਤਾਤੀ ਵਾਉ ਨ ਲਾਇਓ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kantth laae apuno daas raakhio taatee vaau na laaeo |1|
lines.english: Hugging him close in His Embrace, the Lord saves and protects His slave. The hot winds cannot even touch him. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਾ ਅਨੰਦ ਕਰਹੁ ਦਾਸ ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਨਾਨਕ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸੁ ਮਨਿ ਆਇਓ ॥੨॥੧੪॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: mahaa anand karahu daas har ke naanak bisvaas man aaeo |2|14|18|
lines.english: The Lord's slaves enjoy ultimate ecstasy; O Nanak, faith has welled up in my mind. ||2||14||18||
Raag Malaar, Fifth Mehl, Chau-Padhay, Second House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
The Gurmukh reflects on the Sound-current of the Naad, and the wisdom of the Vedas.
The Gurmukh knows that the universe is the extension of the three gunas, the three dispositions.
Without the Perfect Guru, there is only pitch-black darkness. ||1||
The Gurmukh sees God pervading everywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਵਸਿਓ ਸਾਸਿ ਗਿਰਾਸਿ ਅਪਣਾ ਖਸਮੁ ਧਿਆਈਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur upades har hiradai vasio saas giraas apanaa khasam dhiaaeeai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Following the Guru's Teachings, the Lord comes to dwell within the heart; I meditate on my Lord and Master with every breath and morsel of food. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਮਨ ਗੁਰੁ ਗੁਰੁ ਕਰਤ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: mere man gur gur karat sadaa sukh paaeeai |
lines.english: O my mind, calling on the Guru, eternal peace is found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਨਿਤ ਗਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur ke gun anadin nit gaau |
lines.english: Night and day, I continually sing the Glorious Praises of the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਰਣ ਵਿਟਹੁ ਬਲਿ ਜਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur ke charan vittahu bal jaau |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice to the Guru's Feet.
I take my cleansing bath in the dust of the Guru's Feet.
I am honored in the True Court of the Lord. ||2||
The Guru is the boat, to carry me across the terrifying world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਿ ਭੇਟਿਐ ਨ ਹੋਇ ਜੋਨਿ ਅਉਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: gur bhettiai na hoe jon aautaar |
lines.english: Meeting with the Guru, I shall not be reincarnated ever again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਕਰਮਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਧੁਰਿ ਆਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jaa kau karam likhiaa dhur aae |3|
lines.english: who has such karma inscribed on his forehead by the Primal Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee sevaa so jan paae |
lines.english: That humble being serves the Guru,
The Guru is my life; the Guru is my support.
The Guru is my way of life; the Guru is my family.
The Guru is my Lord and Master; I seek the Sanctuary of the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਜਾ ਕੀ ਕੀਮ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥੪॥੧॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: naanak gur paarabraham jaa kee keem na paaee |4|1|19|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Guru is the Supreme Lord God; His value cannot be estimated. ||4||1||19||
Malaar, Fifth Mehl:
I enshrine the Lord's Feet within my heart;

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਨੇ ਸੇਵਕ ਕਉ ਲਏ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: apane sevak kau le prabh laae |
lines.english: God enjoins His servant to his tasks.
in His Mercy, God has united me with Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੀ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਕਹੀ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: taa kee keemat kahee na jaae |1|
lines.english: His worth cannot be expressed. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮੑਰੀ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਤੇ ਤੂੰ ਚਿਤਿ ਆਵਹਿ ਆਠ ਪਹਰ ਤੇਰੈ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tumaree kripaa te toon chit aaveh aatth pehar terai rang raate |1| rahaau |
lines.english: By Your Grace, You come to mind; I am imbued with Your Love, twenty-four hours a day. ||1||Pause||
Please be merciful to me, O Perfect Giver of peace.
One who understands the Hukam of Your Command is absorbed in Truth.
Singing and listening, it is all by Your Will.
Without You, there is no place at all. ||2||
Pain and pleasure come by Your Command, O Creator Lord.
Chanting and meditating on Your Name, I live.
You are the Creator of both realms.
I am a sacrifice to Your Glorious Grandeur. ||3||
By the Pleasure of Your Will You forgive, and by the Pleasure of Your Will You award punishment.
You alone know Your value.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਆਪੇ ਬੂਝਹਿ ਸੁਣਿ ਆਪਿ ਵਖਾਣਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: too aape boojheh sun aap vakhaaneh |
lines.english: You alone understand, You Yourself speak and listen.
They alone are devotees, who are pleasing to Your Will.
